title: Cozy Polish Kabocha Tagine
description: A hearty and comforting Polish dish featuring kabocha squash, cooked in a tagine style with a blend of spices, in an iron teppan vessel, perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan vessel over medium heat.
Add the olive oil and swirl it around to coat the bottom of the vessel.
Add the caraway seeds and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, pepper, and thyme, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cubed kabocha squash and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the vessel, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the squash is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
